title.zh: 在墓道尽头保存人性残响的夜行族
title.en: The Night People Beneath the Last Cemetery

summary.zh: 犬面、蹄足的地下食尸族群，从墓园通往幻梦境；人类可能逐渐成为他们，他们也可能保留旧日情谊。
summary.en: Dog-faced subterranean eaters of the dead who travel from cemeteries into Dreamland and may once have been human.

description.zh:
洛夫克拉夫特笔下的食尸鬼住在墓园、地窟与幻梦境的地下通道中，形似橡胶般皮肤包裹的犬面人形，脚掌近蹄，以死者为食。《皮克曼的模特》先让画家把它们带进波士顿地下；《梦寻秘境卡达斯》则让已经完成转变的皮克曼成为族群中的向导，率领同伴帮助伦道夫·卡特。

它们恐怖，却不完全属于恶。食尸鬼遵守友谊、交换情报，也会在关键时刻冒险救援。人类与它们之间还存在可渗透的界线：长期迷恋墓穴、模仿其生活的人，可能逐渐变成其中一员。这样的设定让“怪物”不再只是门外来客，而是一种社会和身体的迁徙。食尸鬼位于克苏鲁神话较少见的灰色地带——死亡是它们的食粮，忠诚却仍然有效；坟墓是文明排除之物的终点，也成了另一条道路的入口。
description.en:
Lovecraft’s ghouls inhabit cemeteries, crypts, and the underground passages of Dreamland. They are dog-faced humanoids with rubbery skin and hooflike feet, feeding upon the dead. “Pickman’s Model” first brings them beneath Boston through a painter’s canvases; The Dream-Quest of Unknown Kadath reveals that Pickman has completed his transformation and now acts as a guide among them, leading his companions to aid Randolph Carter.

They are horrifying without being simply evil. Ghouls keep friendships, exchange information, and accept danger in a rescue. The line between humanity and their community is permeable: a person who haunts graves and imitates their life may gradually become one of them. Monstrosity becomes a social and bodily migration rather than an intruder waiting outside the door. Ghouls occupy an uncommon grey province in the mythos. Death is their food, but loyalty continues to matter; the grave, where civilization deposits what it excludes, opens into a road traveled by another people.

value.zh: 《皮克曼的模特》《梦寻秘境卡达斯》
value.en: “Pickman’s Model” and The Dream-Quest of Unknown Kadath

value.zh: 画家理查德·厄普顿·皮克曼

sources.zh: H. P. 洛夫克拉夫特《皮克曼的模特》
